description: Testmaxxing Formation v2 integration: pub/sub channel visualization, cross-wave data-flow edges, DOT graph rendering, and notification channel filtering in APM dashboard.

userStories.title: Add edge_type to D3 formation graph edges
userStories.description: Extend the formation graph edge schema to support typed edges: hierarchy (solid black), pubsub (dashed blue), aggregation (dashed green), and data_export (bold orange). The JS hook renders each type with distinct stroke/dash/color.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge schema includes edge_type field: hierarchy|pubsub|aggregation|data_export
push_formation_graph/2 emits edge_type on each edge
formation_graph.js renders hierarchy edges as solid black lines
formation_graph.js renders pubsub edges as dashed blue lines
formation_graph.js renders aggregation edges as dashed green lines
formation_graph.js renders data_export edges as bold orange lines
Existing formations without edge_type default to hierarchy
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Add channel field to notification schema and API
userStories.description: Add optional channel field to POST /api/notify for pub/sub channel routing. Store in ETS notification record. Expose in notification JSON responses.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/notify accepts optional channel field
channel stored in notification ETS record
channel included in notification JSON responses
Notifications panel shows channel badge when present
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Add pub/sub channel edges to formation tree builder
userStories.description: When building the formation tree, detect agents with publishes/subscribes metadata and generate pubsub-typed edges alongside hierarchy edges. Detect cross-wave exports and generate data_export edges.
acceptanceCriteria: build_formation_tree/2 generates pubsub edges from agent publishes/subscribes metadata
Squadron lead aggregation edges rendered as aggregation type
Cross-wave exports (agent metadata exports/imports fields) rendered as data_export edges
Existing formations without pub/sub metadata unchanged
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: DOT graph rendering endpoint and FormationLive tab
userStories.description: Add GET /api/v2/formations/:id/dot endpoint returning Graphviz DOT source. Add a DOT/PNG tab in FormationLive that renders via server-side dot command or displays raw DOT source.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v2/formations/:id/dot returns DOT source as text/plain
DOT generation uses formation agents + edges from build_formation_tree
FormationLive has a Graph tab showing rendered PNG or raw DOT
If graphviz (dot) is installed, render to PNG inline
If graphviz not installed, show raw DOT with copy button
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Notification panel channel filter
userStories.description: Add a channel filter dropdown to the notifications panel. When a channel is selected, only notifications matching that channel are shown. Include source filter for testmaxxing-pubsub grouping.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification panel has channel filter dropdown populated from distinct channels in current notifications
Selecting a channel filters notification list in real-time
Source filter allows filtering by source field (e.g. testmaxxing-pubsub)
Clear filter resets to all notifications
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Agent registration accepts publishes/subscribes/exports/imports metadata
userStories.description: Extend POST /api/register to accept and store publishes, subscribes, exports, and imports fields in agent metadata. These drive pub/sub edge generation in the formation tree.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/register accepts publishes (list of channel names)
POST /api/register accepts subscribes (list of channel names)
POST /api/register accepts exports (list of export keys)
POST /api/register accepts imports (list of import keys with source)
Fields stored in agent ETS record metadata
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Testmaxxing formation template in UpmStore
userStories.description: Add a built-in testmaxxing formation template to UpmStore that pre-defines the 20-agent hierarchy, channel schema, and cross-wave exports. Used when deploying via /testmaxxing or POST /api/formations with template=testmaxxing.
acceptanceCriteria: UpmStore has testmaxxing template with 20 agents across 5 squadrons
Template includes pub/sub channel schema for all 17 channels
Template includes cross-wave exports (bravo.auth_session_cookie)
POST /api/formations with template=testmaxxing creates full formation
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: FormationLive legend for edge types
userStories.description: Add a visual legend to FormationLive showing edge type meanings: hierarchy (solid), pub/sub callback (dashed blue), aggregation (dashed green), data export (bold orange).
acceptanceCriteria: Legend component renders below or beside the D3 graph
Shows 4 edge types with matching colors and dash patterns
Legend is collapsible to save space
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Integration tests for typed edges, channel notifications, DOT endpoint
userStories.description: Test suite covering: typed edge rendering data, channel notification filtering, DOT generation endpoint, and testmaxxing template deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests for push_formation_graph with typed edges
Tests for notification channel field storage and filtering
Tests for GET /api/v2/formations/:id/dot response
Tests for testmaxxing template formation creation
All tests pass: mix test --only testmaxxing

userStories.title: Final compile gate and LILY-554 closure
userStories.description: Final mix compile --warnings-as-errors, manual browser verification of formation graph with typed edges, and update LILY-554 in Plane to Done.
acceptanceCriteria: mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes
mix test --only testmaxxing passes
Formation graph visually shows typed edges in browser
Notification channel filter works in dashboard
LILY-554 marked Done in Plane (or moved to CCEM namespace)
